Ferdinand Verbiest (1623вҖ“88) in his *K'un yГј t'u ЕЎwo* еқӨијҝең–иӘӘ, and
was hence adopted in the pharmacopЕ“ia of the Chinese, for it figures
in the *Pen ts'ao kan mu ЕЎi i.*В№ The Chinese Gazetteer of MacaoВІ mentions
*pa'r-su-ma* aromatic е·ҙзҲҫй…Ҙйә»йҰҷ as a kind of benjoin. In this case
we have a transcription of Portuguese *bГЎlsamo*.
footnotes: В№ Ch. 6, p. 19. See, further, Watters, Essays on the Chinese Language, p. 339.
